title: anisomeles grandibractea
intro: Anisomeles grandibractea, also known as the white flower bush or Thai basil, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to be effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Anisomeles grandibractea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, among others. Anisomeles grandibractea can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
